items.why: Another classic faux ami. Spanish 'constipado' refers to nasal congestion (a cold). The medical 'constipated' is 'estreñido'. Confusion can lead to awkward medical conversations.

items.q: What is the Spanish word for 'mobile phone' (in Spain)?
items.misconception: El celular (which is used in Latin America).
items.why: Vocabulary varies by region. Spain: móvil, ordenador (computer), coche (car). Latin America: celular, computadora, carro/auto. Students mix these. Examiners accept both, but consistency matters. Móvil is most common in GCSE/A-level.

items.q: Why does 'Me gusta el chocolate' literally translate as 'Chocolate is pleasing to me'?
items.correct: Gustar works backwards - the thing liked is the subject, the person is the indirect object.
items.misconception: Gustar means 'to like' and works exactly like English.
items.random_wrong: It is a typo for 'Yo gusto'.
items.why: Students translate gustar as 'to like' with English structure. Actually gustar = 'to be pleasing to'. So 'me gustan los libros' (books are pleasing to me) - the verb agrees with the thing, not the liker.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my sister'?
items.correct: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.misconception: Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.
items.random_wrong: Yo llevo bien con mi hermana.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Me llevo bien con mi hermana.. “Me llevo bueno con mi hermana.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is the correct use of ser/estar: 'I am tired'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Estoy cansado.. “Soy cansado.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many forms does the Spanish adjective 'simpático' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4 forms. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
